entry.title: Re: Is BI Support Tool supported on SAP BusinessObjects B...
entry.content: The URL is not functional anymore. Care to share the document number or the latest URL please?

entry.title: Set a default hierarchy
entry.content:
Let us assume to have a Datasphere Analytical Model where at least one field has "hierarchies with directory" hierarchy related. Let us assume to have all the required associations, in the Analytical Model, to have all these hierarchies available for selection.

My question is: is there a way to flag, one of these hierarchies as the default hierarchy? As far as I see, the default hierarchy is always the flat hierarchy. Nevertheless, my goal is to let the user to choose his own default hierarchy, in such a way that, when he opens the Analytical Model and select the field for the row or column section, the system already does the preselection on the defaulted hierarchy.

I hope to have been able to explain my doubt and what I am trying to do.

Thanks in advance for your help

Regards

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Issue with Availability/ATP despite confirmed qty in...
entry.content: From SD point of view, you should check in co09 instead of md04.  Please check in co09 whether unrestricted stock is available.

entry.title: Re: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module - Standard Process
entry.content: @DavidSomelofske: Many thanks for your comment and help! As you wrote "different purposes". We want to use the Overall Performance Rating, which is stored in the Talent Profile for Succession Management and Career Planning. Furthermore, we created a separate Performance-Template in order to have a separate Performance Rating (which is not stored in the Talent Profile) for Compensation Module. As both are Overall Performance Ratings, my questions would be if you would consider my described scenarios as 2 different purposes. - Many thanks

entry.title: Re: Overall Performance Rating for Compensation Module -...

We want to use the Overall Performance Rating, which is stored in the Talent Profile for Succession Management and Career Planning. Furthermore, we created a separate Performance-Template in order to have a separate Performance Rating (which is not stored in the Talent Profile) for the Compensation Module. As both are Overall Performance Ratings, my questions would be if you would consider my both described scenarios as 2 different purposes. - Many thanks and Best Regards, Joachim

Could you please help us on how to achieve this functionality ?

Regards,

Prakash

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....
entry.content:
Hi ,

which DMEE data did you use to customize Pain 09? in Attachements of S.N 2253571 have 7 XMLs that SAP did not accept, because they did not started with DMEE tree and they had not nodes when I try to use them. Can you share your Knowledge.  I have to customize it and now I stuck in DMEE!

thanks

entry.title: Honored to be part of HCII 2025 in Gothenborg!
entry.content:
Every once in a while in a student's academic journey, a moment comes that transcends the usual grind, a milestone that marks a significant ascent in learning and achievement. Recently, I experienced such a moment: my thesis was accepted as a Poster Paper at the prestigious 27th International Conference on Human–Computer Interaction (HCII 2025) in Gothenburg, Sweden, held from June 22–27. Although I participated virtually, the experience was truly impactful.
Introduction to HCII 2025

The HCII conference is one of the leading global forums dedicated to Human–Computer Interaction, User Experience (UX), and interaction design. Its reputation for creating a dynamic space where researchers and practitioners from all over the world gather to exchange ideas and explore the latest trends is truly remarkable. Attending this conference was an exciting experience  for me, offering an unparalleled opportunity to engage with pioneers in the field.


My Thesis: A Brief Overview

Although I couldn't attend the conference in person, my mentor presented my paper on-site, for which I am very grateful. My thesis, titled "Manually and GPT-4 Created Feedback Questionnaires in Unmoderated Studies: A Comparative Case Study," examines the extent to which GPT-4 can support user researchers in creating questionnaires and evaluates the quality of the generated results. With this study, I hope to contribute to making the work of user researchers more efficient and increasing the use of AI in everyday work.

👉 If you’re interested, you can find the paper published in the HCII 2025 proceedings here:
https://lnkd.in/eBHZHfRb 


Immersing in the Conference: Highlights and Learnings

The opportunity to attend various online sessions at the HCII provided a wealth of knowledge. From presentations on the role of artificial intelligence in human-computer interaction (HCI) to innovative applications of AI, the conference offered a broad spectrum of insights. I found the session on Virtual Agents and UX Design for Personalized Health Support particularly interesting, as it addressed practical applications of virtual avatars. I was impressed by how many relevant and previously under-researched ideas exist, each of which would be worth pursuing in more detail.


Despite the challenges of virtual participation, the conference offered numerous opportunities to network with other conference attendees and researchers. For example, the community chat enabled intensive discussions on topics of mutual interest and encouraged the exchange of ideas. The Q&A sessions after each presentation also contributed to new perspectives and comprehensive insights into the topics. It's fascinating to see how many people are critically questioning things, actively considering complex topics, and showing interest in the future of human-computer interaction.

Reflection and Future Aspirations

Reflecting on this experience, I am deeply grateful to everyone who supported me throughout this journey. From mentors to peers, each played a vital role in helping me hone my ideas and present them effectively. Looking forward, I hope this achievement opens many doors for further learning, collaboration, and growth. 
In conclusion, participating in HCII 2025 was an unforgettable experience, marked by learning, networking, and the thrill of presenting my work to an esteemed audience. It serves as a valuable stepping stone in my academic career, inspiring me to continue exploring and innovating. I highly recommend everyone visit HCI if they get the chance. Whether as an exhibitor or a visitor, your perspective and horizons will be broadened.


Thank you to everyone who has supported me on this path. Here's to more exciting possibilities and transformative discoveries ahead! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Configure Relative Dates in Manage Route Maps for a 3...
entry.content: Thanks Jordi. The settings are enabled, however I'm not sure which of the drop-down options to choose within the map. For Example, do I choose X days After: form creation date, same step start date, previous step exit date, or previous step due date?

entry.title: Re: Down Payment at header level of a Sales Order
entry.content: Billing plan processing is item-level only in standard SAP SD. Header-level configuration of billing plan types is just a defaulting mechanism, not actual functionality. You must maintain billing plans and down payments at the item level, even if header-level billing plan settings are in place. Even with billing plan customization done at the header level, the down payment request and billing plan logic (like dates, milestones) must still be maintained at the item level. I hope this will help you. Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support. If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better. Appreciate your feedback. Thanks, Divyesh

entry.title: Custom FIORI notification while (MIR7)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I would like to receive a Fiori notification when an invoice is parked using transaction MIR7, without triggering any approval workflow.

Does anyone know the necessary configuration steps to achieve this?

Thanks in advance !

entry.title: Re: Is it safe to activate Business Function LOG_EAM_CI_7...
entry.content:
I always activate this type of Business Function (BF) in a sandbox system first, to analyse the impact (e.g. DDIC changes, FM and method signature changes) and to test it, before installing it in a development system.

If you value your sanity, I suggest you never start with a production system!

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning and Adoption Forum 2025 - Doku, Folien, Videos und mehr
entry.content: Ein herzliches Dankeschön an alle Teilgeber:innen, Teilnehmer:innen und das ganze SAP Team und dich Thomas für diese Summary - der ganze Content ist echt beeindruckend - DANKE!

entry.title: SAP B1 BY-PRODUCT
entry.content:
Hello,
In my SAP B1 version 9.3, I created a bill of materials (BOM) with a negative quantity declaration for the by-product.
My quantity process is correct and everything works fine. My problem is with the cost of my item; it doesn't include the cost of the by-product.
How can I get the cost of my pure product without the by-product charge?

Any idea?

Regards

#sapb1 #sapbusinessone #production

entry.title: Re: New Course Home : Launch Content in New TAB / New WINDOW effect on content communication
entry.content: Hi Karen, we have our content launch method set to launch in a new window because we were told that this was the best method to avoid launch errors. We'd prefer inline launching. Is this now improved?

entry.title: Re: ADOBE Forms does not display custom field contents
entry.content:
Hi @Salim-Satiro,
The implementation is the issue. You cannot select from I_SALESDOCITEMPRICINGELEMENT to get transactional data. If you select from a CDS View, you will always get the data from the database.

During creation of the sales order, there will be nothing on the database and therefore you will not get any data via CDS View.

In change mode of the sales order, you will only get the data from the database which could be outdated, as the data could have been changed.

The BAdIs are also called in display mode which could be confusing as it will show the right values, but they are not persisted.

That is also the reason why you cannot see any data in the Output.

Why do you need the values in custom fields? Are they not available in the output structure (PriceItemConditionNode / PriceTotalConditionNode)?

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: 2820775 – Source of Information for Field in structur...
entry.content:
Bonjour,

Est-il possible également de recevoir ce fichier ?

Merci d'avance

Romain

entry.title: Re: The Future Has Been Written – the Rise of the Database Administrator
entry.content:
Hi There,

I’m reaching out to request assistance regarding a challenge I’m facing with the C_DBADM_2404 certification.

Unfortunately, I have attempted the exam three times and narrowly missed the passing score on two occasions, scoring 68% (with the pass mark being 69%). I understand that SAP policy limits candidates to three attempts per exam version, and as a result, I’m now blocked from retaking this certification until the next version is released.

However, I still have three exam attempts remaining on my subscription, which is valid until the end of August 2025. I’ve also invested heavily in study materials specifically for C_DBADM_2404 and was unaware of the three-attempt policy until I was blocked. This situation is deeply concerning for me, as obtaining this certification is essential for my current role, and my job is now at risk.

I’ve reached out to SAP Support requesting either a manual review of my last exam attempt or a possible additional attempt, but was informed that no exceptions can be made.

Given the urgency, I kindly ask:

  1. When is the next version (e.g., C_DBADM_2504) expected to be released?

  2. Is there any available alternative certification that closely matches the C_DBADM_2404 content where I could make use of my current preparation and remaining attempts?

Your advice and support would be greatly appreciated during this critical time.

entry.title: Re: BYD2405 Connection to HRMC
entry.content: Hi, I am currently trying to integrate SAP BYD with HMRC. I have scoped the solution and am now in the step where i need to enter authorization Code. Can I know which User ID and PW did you use? Did you use end user HMRC user id and PW? Your response will be of great help !

entry.title: Re: 2820775 – Source of Information for Field in structur...
entry.content:
You have to download the 3 files in a same directory, then open the zip one.

  • You require a S-id (proof be a Sap customer of partner) to open the note for legal reason.

entry.title: High Volume of data observed in table ADRV in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello all,

We are on SAP S/4HANA Version 2020 on-premises. We have observed high volume of data in table ADRV. There are around 10 million records currently in ADRV out of which application table EKPO itself has 8.4 million records. Out of these 8 million records are present only for contracts.  Each contract line is registering nearly 5000 to 5500 records. We are aware that ADRV is like a where-used table for addresses. However, we see the number of records is very high in this table for EKPO. For TM activated POs, this table is accessed by the system during release process (Flexible Workflow through BADI) and the approval process is ending with error "Memory shortage". 

We have below queries:

 

1. Is this a normal situation for ADRV to have such a huge volume of data or is it a bug in SAP?

2. Also have anyone of you noticed that addresses of contracts lines are captured in ADRV in such a high volume. 

3. We want to know if anybody has come across this situation where the approval workflow is ending in error due to lack of system memory and if yes how have you overcome this in your environment?

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best Regards,

Nithin Rao

entry.title: FSCM credit worklist for single BP.
entry.content: Usually credit worklist program executed whole credit segment which may have 1000's of BP and take long long time to finish. Is it possible to execute the program only for one BP?

entry.title: Service PO With Multiple Line Items Failing on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing issue while sending a service purchase order (containing multiple line items) to the supplier on SAP Business Network. The product is SAP Business Network Commerce Automation, Foundation Option.

The purchase order is created and successfully sent from SAP S/4HANA and reaches Managed Gateway (CIG) where it gets failed during conversion to cXML with the following error:

Please note that this is happening only in case of service PO with multiple line items and working fine for service PO with single line item, material PO with single as well as multiple line items.

 

Can anyone please guide what might be the root cause?

SAP Business Network for Suppliers  SAP digital supplier network membership  SAP digital supplier network membership for Supply Chain  SAP Integration Suite SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network SAP Business Network for Logistics

entry.title: Preferential Origin – For externally procured configurable materials
entry.content:
We have a requirement wherein the client would like to understand how System determines the Preferential Origin for externally procured configurable materials. These materials should be treated similarly to other externally procured items — i.e., preferential origin should be determined based on supplier-provided documentation, such as a preferential origin certificate or LTSD.

Issue: Configurable materials-externally procured in Sales orders are not being transferred from ECC to GTS system.

Reason: Material BoM is not being maintained for plant 1000 Tcode- CS03, which causes BOM – Explosion issue.

If we check the table entries for BoM in the table MAST for plant 1000, we can see that system does not give any result (because BoM is not maintained for this product for Plant 1000 – in CS03).

Is there any way to achieve this requirement using Custom development especially if you have done it in your respective project??

Regards, Mayank

entry.title: Issue During Testing – Debugger Shows Blank Screen When Breakpoint is Reached
entry.content:
I give approval for SAP Support to use these Steps to Reproduce, while connected to my impacted non-production environments, even if the steps result in a change being made and my approval remains valid until the issue is resolved, unless I inform SAP Support otherwise.

 

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Need Partner Support? Here’s Who to Contact and How
entry.content:
Different ways SAP Partner Support can be reached.

Via Live chat or the Contact Form on the Partner Portal

Live Chat enables partners to reach the Partner Support Team in real time, ensuring faster responses and issue resolution for less complex queries.

Using our Partner Contact form ensures faster, more accurate support by capturing key details upfront, streamlining routing, and enabling better tracking for quicker resolution.

 

Log on to SAP Partner Portal | welcome and select 'Get help' on the bottom right hand side of the page where you will be able to select Live chat or the Contact form.

Site can also be accessed via https://partneredge.sap.com/en/public/get-help.html

Via SAP for Me

Partners can also easily access support through the Get Assistance section in their SAP for Me application, making SAP for Me the one stop shop for their partnership with SAP.

Contact the SAP Partner Support by using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard via SAP For ME.

Not Yet a Partner and interested in finding out more information – Please Contact Us here

Not able to log into Partner Edge – Please Contact Us here – (Please note to ensure to select “Partner” in relationship to SAP selection box.

This blog was written in collaboration with my colleague Kenny Laffey. 

Thanks for reading and hope you found it helpful! 

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: Seeking Architectural Guidance for a Generic Data Mai...
entry.content:
Hi Marian,

I asked AI to gave me a minimal RAP step by step example. I failed at step 3.
The Behavior Definition shows following error: "Empty Module #66"
The conclusion of AI is that SAP BW/4HANA 2023 even with the most recent support package has an old Netweaver version and therefore no ABAP Platform 2021 features. And with that BW/4HANA has no RAP stack at all.
My conclusion now is RAP on BW/4HANA (on-premise) is not limited, it is not working at all, if I could not get a Hello World program to work.

STEP 1: SE11 - Creating a simple table
@EndUserText.label : 'Hello World with RAP'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zhello_rap {

key mandt : abap.clnt not null;
key id : abap.numc(5) not null;
name : abap.char(40);
created_at : abap.dats;

}

STEP 2: Creating a CDS Data Definition
@EndUserText.label: 'Hello RAP View'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@ObjectModel.modelCategory: #BUSINESS_OBJECT
@ObjectModel.createEnabled: true
@ObjectModel.updateEnabled: true
@ObjectModel.deleteEnabled: true
define root view entity Z_I_HELLO_RAP as select from zhello_rap {
key id,
name,
created_at
}

STEP 3: Creating a CDS Behavior Definition
define behavior for Z_I_HELLO_RAP
persistent table zhello_rap
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
{
create;
update;
delete;
}

entry.title: Add Custom field with Custom fields app and enable the usage to SOAP API in S/4 HANA On-premise
entry.content:
Hi, 

I added custom fields for business context Sales: Sales Document and enabled the usage for service interface: sales order replicate A2A  operation: SalesOrderbulkreplication_OUT

Once it is published custom field is added in the SOAP API but with the different schema format <xsd: group ref > instead of <xsd:element name > ( for standard fields schema format ).
Why is it different that standard? 


Thanks

entry.title: Re: While printing a Form LOGO is missing for the new com...
entry.content:
Hello @Sanjay_BS1 

Thanks for the clarification. Since your voucher printing form is a custom template managed via Form Service by Adobe on SAP BTP, it’s not controlled by the Maintain Logos app in S4 Public Cloud.

To update the logo,

You need to embed the logo directly into the form layout

Then re-upload the updated form to Form Service by Adobe in BTP

The Maintain Logos app only applies to standard forms managed within the Public Cloud Output Management framework.

Let me know if you need help updating the form or accessing it in BTP.

Best regards,
Chuma,

entry.title: Re: Creating Mashup to Show SAP S/4HANA Addresses in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities Add-o

Could you please outline the steps required to generate a URL link for the HTML code provided, so that it can be updated in the "Create HTML Mashup" screen of Service Cloud V2?

Additionally, could you recommend the best application or method to generate such a URL link for HTML code?

entry.title: Make JobInfo Field Mandatory
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

There is a field in the Job Information section that also appears during the Add New Employee process in SF Employee Central. I need to make this field mandatory, as it is crucial for a subsequent integration job to function correctly. The field is of type string and uses a picklist.
I’ve updated the configuration in BCUI to set the field as mandatory, but this has had no effect — the field still lacks a red asterisk, and the system allows submission without it being filled in.
Could you please advise on how to properly enforce this requirement?

Kind regards,
A.

entry.title: Re: Handling Cash Advances with SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service v4 API's
entry.content: @Ramshankar1136   I believe the accounting for the issuance of the cash advance is in the employee's reimbursement currency so it is expected that the repayment is also journaled in the reimbursement currency.  You could always get back to the incurred currency by looking in the expenseEntry section.

entry.title: Re: Need Partner Support? Here’s Who to Contact and How
entry.content: Great blog well done to all involved!

entry.title: Re: High Volume of data observed in table ADRV in SAP S/4...
entry.content: Never get this problem, but you could look at Business Address Services common issues (BC-SRV-ADR) of Support Content .

entry.title: Re: Local amounts in BSEG do not match / add each other o...
entry.content:
Hi @anne_mielke ,

Dr/Cr mismatch might be due to couple of reasons what i can think of:-

1. Exchange rates used at the time of WIP posting may have differed significantly between debit and credit lines. Verify your exchange rates.

2. Secondly, during settlement, someone might have overwritten exchange rate in KO88. This can cuase to deviate amounts substantially.

3. Also check your currency configuration in Ob22 and Ob08. Valuation logic for local currency might be misaligned, especially if you're using multiple currency types

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Handling Cash Advances with SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service v4 API's
entry.content:
Hi @tim_chapman24 

Thanks for your response. If the client is accounting the cash advance in the requested foreign currency, then is there a way to account the unused cash advance return as well in the requested foreign currency?

For example, the user's reimbursement currency is "Euro" and if the cash advance is requested and posted in USD and is there a way to return the unused cash advance back in USD? As per the JSON, the foreign amount is not captured for cash advance returns.

Is there a way to handle this requirement?

Thanks a lot for your support!

Regards,

Ramshankar

entry.title: Re: SAP BW ANALYSIS
entry.content: Could you help me with this topic, please?

entry.title: Re: ADOBE Forms does not display custom field contents
entry.content: Hi Christina Thanks for the replying Perhaps I have not mentioned before but even if no BADI is in place, the custom fields populates manually do not show in the output layout. The idea of using custom fields is to prepare the data that need to be printed in the layout. Why we had to do it ? The data from pricingelements regarding taxes do not appear in the report. People from SAP have recommended this approach through BADI + changing the output layout.

entry.title: Re: 2820775 – Source of Information for Field in structur...

entry.title: Auto Import to PRE-PRD - in ChaRM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We want to configure auto import to PRE-PRD in ChaRM when the status is changed from QAS to PRE-PRD. 

Is it recommended to schedule a auto import into PRE PRD when the status is changed? What are the disadvantages if we configure the auto import to PRE-PRD when status changed from "To be tested in QAS " to import to PRE-PRD.

Please advice.

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: Re: Custom WDV Depreciation Key for Depreciation Area 15...
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am facing an issue related to the depreciation calculation for Depreciation Area 15, where we are using the INWC depreciation key with a cut-off percentage of 5%. To test the 180-day depreciation rule (as per Indian Income Tax regulations), I created a sample Asset Block using a dummy block key (9999) and assigned assets to it. I then performed an acquisition for ₹5,00,000 dated 01.11.2025. As per the 180-day rule, since the acquisition is made after September 30th, the system should calculate only 50% of the depreciation rate (i.e., 5% on ₹5,00,000 assuming a full rate of 10%).

However, the system is currently calculating depreciation incorrectly — it does not align with the expected 180-day logic. Could you please help confirm: Are there any specific configuration steps we might have missed to enable the 180-day rule in this scenario? Is there a recommended report where we can view the depreciation values and confirm the income tax-specific calculations (WDV, % applied, etc.)?

Appreciate your help in resolving this issue.

Regards

Aswathy M

entry.title: Re: ADOBE Forms does not display custom field contents
entry.content:
Hi @Salim-Satiro,
If you enter a value manually, it will also be available in output management data provider. 

There is a blog post / guide how to use key user extensibility in Sales. That guide includes a step by step description how to create a custom form template and add a custom field to it. That custom field is also calculated in a Cloud BAdI. Please check if you have followed all the required steps.
In addition, you can find the official documentation here.

If there is an issue with the output, please raise an incident for SD-SLS-GF-OC-2CL.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Roles in GRC returning from Production to Development...
entry.content: This issue is coming in single role maintenance. I have updated the parameter 3005 to NO and the role keeps returning to Development after the update. I even have parameter 3004 (Default Role Status) as PRD.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding Custom Task Permission Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

I assume that you are referring to custom New Hire task (?).

If so, you can look into Primary and Secondary new hire taks. Configuring Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks Step for Onboarding Programs

This will allow to trigger different New Hire tasks at different stages.

Kind Regards,
Noemi

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2505 Now Available – What’s New?
entry.content:
The latest release of SAP Service and Asset Manager brings a range of new features and improvements for technicians, supervisors, and logistics teams. This update introduces AI capability, streamlined workflows, expanded logistics, new configuration tools, and improved user experiences.

Highlights in this Release

  • Hands-free job completion with AI-driven voice-to-text
  • Deeper SAP Field Service Management (FSM) integration
  • Extended support for warehouse and field logistics
  • Modernized user interface and usability improvements
  • Backend enhancements

Please read on to learn more.

Key features delivered in the 2505 release

AI-Supported Voice-to-Text Job Completion*

Our first AI-powered feature lets field technicians speak their job details directly into the app, which transcribes their voice into structured data that automatically fills relevant fields—such as hours worked, expenses, or mileage. This hands-free approach lets technicians capture important information quickly and naturally, without needing to type.

Once the data is populated, technicians can review and adjust the entries before finalizing the job. This streamlined process not only speeds up job completion but also improves accuracy by reducing missed details, ultimately helping teams maintain high-quality data and work more efficiently.

Advanced FSM Integrations

The 2505 release includes several SAP Field Service Management integrations that enhance collaboration and workforce management.

Field Service Crew Support

Support activities assigned to crews in SAP Field Service Management. Crew leads can change the status of service orders and handle crew assignments from mobile devices, while supporting technicians can collaborate with crew members to perform all service-related tasks.

FSM Crowd Service Integration†

Find additional technicians and assign operations to external technicians. External technicians can perform operations directly from the SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile application; sync time, material, and expenses details.

Supporting Vehicle Stock in Field Service with SAP S/4HANA Service

Add existing stock to an order, transfer stock to vehicles, and view recent transfers directly within the app, ensuring efficient inventory management.

Smartform Flexibility

Technicians can add new FSM Smartforms‡ to existing operations. They can create Smartforms from Templates and attach them to a variety of objects, i.e., equipments, functional locations, service orders, and order items.

Expanded Logistics and Warehouse Support

Your logistics teams gain mobile access for core processes with support for new personas — with direct integration into key SAP solutions to meet enterprise needs.

Warehouse Clerk Persona§

Supports warehouse operations such as task assignment and confirmation, resource management, exception handling, and physical inventory counts. Includes handling of serialized and batch-managed parts, pick handling units, and bulk task confirmations — fully integrated with SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Field Logistics Operator Persona§

Enables field logistics activities including remote plant receiving, voyage status updates, and the processing of containers, packages, and delivery items. Includes ad hoc search, single-item and bulk receiving, edit actions, and release steps — integrated with SAP Field Logistics (Transportation Management and EWM).

These capabilities bring critical execution steps to mobile, helping teams move faster, reduce errors, and improve inventory visibility — wherever the work happens.

Enhanced Workflow Support for Maintenance Technicians

Maintenance notification workflows are now more flexible with the Mobile Application Integration Framework (MAIF). This allows mapping of mobile statuses to customized workflows, ensuring technicians follow standardized, compliant steps in the field.

Additionally, users can create dynamic form instances directly from templates within the app, attaching these forms to work orders, operations, or suboperations for streamlined data capture and process consistency.

User Experience Enhancements

The 2505 release introduces several improvements designed to make the app more intuitive, consistent, and efficient for users in the field.

Multi-Sort Filtering 

Filter pages now include separate tabs for filtering and sorting, giving users greater control over how data is organized. Technicians can select multiple sort criteria, reorder them, and choose ascending or descending order—making it easier to surface the information they need.

Modernized Top Action Bar 

We implemented the new FioriActionBar that modernizes the top toolbar, aligning it with SAP Fiori design principles for a cleaner, more consistent user experience. This update improves overflow menu behavior and refines search functionality—making navigation and data input smoother and more intuitive.

Required Field Indicators

All mandatory fields are now clearly marked with an asterisk (*), providing better guidance during data entry and reducing the risk of missed information.

These enhancements improve usability without adding complexity—supporting faster, more confident interactions throughout the app.

Backend Enhancements and Performance Improvements

Several new tools and features are now available to improve performance, streamline configuration, and simplify onboarding for SAP Service and Asset Manager deployments.

Configuration Variants for Multi-Deployment Scenarios 

Support for configuration variants enables more flexible multi-deployment setups across business units. A variant is a reusable set of configuration changes—such as feature toggles, oMDO settings, and value filters—that can be layered on top of the standard configuration at runtime.

Delta Sync Optimization 

A new feature for optimized Mobile Data Object (oMDO) synchronization significantly reduces delta sync duration. This helps speed up data updates between mobile devices and the backend system, especially for large datasets.

Faster Onboarding with SAP BTP Booster

A new SAP BTP Booster is available to simplify app onboarding. It automates the creation of a Cloud Foundry-enabled subaccount and initial configuration for SAP Service and Asset Manager. While backend connections still require manual setup, the booster speeds up the foundational steps for administrators using SAP BTP.

Configuration details

Features described above may be optional and not be default-enabled, and integrations with other SAP products may require additional licensing.

Please refer to Configuring Features and Personas in SAP Service and Asset Manager to learn about assigning personas and visit the Features and Personas Configuration Matrix to see which features are in-scope for each persona .

Release available now

The application is publicly available and includes an offline demo mode for viewing and testing the extensive functionality with maintenance technician, field service technician, safety technician, and inventory clerk personas included.

The application metadata is now available for licensed customers and partners on the SAP Support Portal Software Center and the mobile client may be downloaded from the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

Share your ideas

We encourage you to learn more about submitting improvement requests to influence future releases of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Your ideas are the driving force behind our innovation.

Explore other SAP mobile applications

SAP has a powerful suite of native mobile applications. Follow the MXE Community for latest updates.

—

* AI feature will be available in a future patch release.
† Use of FSM Crowd Integration requires SAP Field Service Management and SAP Crowd Service license.
‡ Use of FSM Smartforms requires SAP Field Service Management license.
§ Use of Warehouse Clerk and Field Logistics Operator requires Extended Warehouse Management and Field Logistics license respectively.

entry.title: SAP Group Reporting - how to handle difference due to rounding of numbers when importing from S4HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

We are connecting S4 HANA to SAP Group reporting (GR) and rounding off numbers, while importing them in GR e.g. instead of 10,549, we are importing 10.55 in Group reporting. This would make overall trial balance unbalanced by 1 or 2, and to correct this a posting of the difference amount would need to be done to a predefined account. We do not want to manually do this but have an automatic solution to address this. 

We were advised to use a Custom Data Monitor step (BADI), but we’d prefer a standard solution without custom development.

Is there any standard functionality in SAP GR that can handle this?

Thanks a lot!

SAP Group Reporting Data Collection 

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

entry.title: Re: Custom logic for checking journal entry submitter aga...
entry.content:
Hi @PatrickNguyen ,

Create implementation scenario for SCENRIO_ID - WS02800046 using Badi - BADI_FCLM_APAR_SZENARIO. We used the same in one of our requirements too. This will trigger during workflow condition.

From logic standpoint, determines the approval level from the amount. Pull approvers from CDS view I_RESPYMGMTTEAMMEMBERAPI. You might need to adjust CDS logic or create a custom view that maps approval levels to user IDs. Compare PARKEDBYUSER (submitter) against that level’s approvers. If the submitter is one of the approvers, it skips the step or else proceed to trigger the approval step.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Monitoring Non-SAP Application Via Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Vitor,

Excellent blog — especially considering the important changes around non-SAP architecture, which many customers still operate. I worked with SAP ITIM integrated with Solution Manager for managing non-SAP systems, but the evolution in this area has been remarkably fast.

Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: How to handle rounding differences when importing numbers from S4 HANA FI
entry.content:
Hello,

We are connecting S4 HANA FI to SAP Group reporting (GR) and rounding off numbers, while importing them in GR e.g. instead of 10,549, we are importing 10.55 in Group reporting. This would make overall trial balance unbalanced by 1 or 2, and to correct this a posting of the difference amount would need to be done to a predefined account. We do not want to manually do this but have an automatic solution to address this. 

We were advised to use a Custom Data Monitor step (BADI), but we’d prefer a standard solution without custom development.

Is there any standard functionality in SAP GR that can handle this?

Thanks a lot!

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting 

SAP Group Reporting Data Collection

entry.title: Re: Text Embedding Service in SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL)
entry.content:
Dear Likun, 

Thank you very much for this great explanation. Do you know whether the PAL_TEXTEMBEDDING procedure is also planned to be made available in SAP HANA Onpremise -> PAL? We would like to use the KNN function in PAL in SAP HANA Onpremise, as described here in the SAP help:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/319d36de4fd64ac3afbf91b1fb3ce8de/c9eeed704f3f4ec39441434db8a874ad.html?locale=en-US&version=2.0.07

Unfortunately, it is not so easy to create a text embedding in SAP HANA Onpremise in order to subsequently determine the distance via KNN. 

Kind regards

Emily

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Composite Roles in launchpad content and layout tools
entry.content:
Thanks for the great blog post!

I have an issue using composite roles. The menu of the comp role is empty. The comp roles just contains single roles (all but one are derived roles) which contain the business catalogs. Space is assigned to the comp role too. Now I want to add apps to the page. However: The "derived from roles" tab is empty. I can manually select the roles and add the apps and they are also working for the user. But in the App Manage Launchpad Pages all the app tiles have an error "out of role context".  Except for the catalog that is not in a derived role. Is that a limitation? Or am I missing something?

entry.title: Re: Heartiest congratulations to our newest SAP Champions in July 2025!
entry.content: Welcome new SAP Champions!

entry.title: Statement Performance Summary not working anymore
entry.content:
For SAP SQL Anywhere there is a tool called SQL Central. Within it you can connect to a database. You can start a "SQL Anywhere Profiler". When running the profiler you can load a "Statement Performance Summary". Within it you can see the top 100 of ran statements. For some reason this summary is empty. It used to work. That is inconvenient, that summary gave us valuable information when analysing slow queries.

It used to work in version "SQLAnywhere 17.0 SP0 PL24 Build 4046", it stopped working version when we upgraded to version "SQL Anywhere 17.0 SP1 PL15 Build 7672".

Is there something in the release notes, upgrade notes, that indicates why this stopped working? If not, how can we be enable it again?

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Solution scenario during implementation pro...
entry.content:
Hello marika_o

 

the Solution Scenario Versions you scoped already you can further use in CALM, will not disappear. Of course you should take a look at the new content and evaluate what of that is relevant for you.

The only option currently available is to create a new Scope including the desired version of the Solution Scenario and manually rescoping the corresponding Solution Processes as well as reassigning (or recreating, in the case of Test Cases) the related project entities.

We are aware that this is inconvenient and we will take your feedback into account for the further development of SAP Cloud ALM. You can also make use of our customer influence program for requesting and tracking your feature request.

 

Best regards

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Problem in calling dynamic actions in BDC
entry.content: how did you resolve the BDC problem , even i am facing the same problem .

entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Warsaw, Poland)
entry.content:
his SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise environment which is available in SAP S/4HANA as of release 2022. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

For this workshop we will provide you with access to system running on release SAP S/4HANA 2023 FSP01.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 19  September 2025 in Warsaw Poland  from 02:00pm - 8:00pm local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location:  KMD Offices,  Inflancka 4A, Warsaw, Poland

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger from SAP.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: How to handle rounding differences when importing num...
entry.content: Read 3331676 - Group Reporting: Rounding selections.

entry.title: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content:
In SAP S4HANA Public Cloud is it possible to attach a document to an unposted invoice (Accounts Payable)

Thanks

entry.title: Enable SSO for BTP platform users
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m trying to set up SSO with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for SAP BTP users in our subaccount. I’ve already:

  • Set up Entra ID with CSI as middelman

  • Successfully tested SSO via the app authentication URL

  • Users can log in to apps using Entra ID

However, I’m stuck on enabling SSO for platform users, users who need access to:

  • The SAP BTP Cockpit

  • Cloud Foundry spaces/orgs/roles

Right now, logging into the Cockpit only shows the default SAP ID Service (no Entra option).

 My questions:

  1. Is it possible to enable SSO (via Entra ID) for BTP platform users?

  2. Can I assign Entra users to Cloud Foundry spaces/orgs?

  3. Do I need IAS in between, or is direct Entra-to-BTP trust enough?

  4. Do I need to activate the Platform Identity Provider service?

Thanks in advance for any guidance or links!

entry.title: Re: Auto-populate Supplier Field in Asset Master When Ass...
entry.content:
Hi @ThanhHaLe ,

Public Cloud does not support automatic populating of the supplier from the PO into the asset master. Upon goods receipt, the system posts the acquisition correctly, but doesn't transfer PO supplier info into the asset master. There is no SSCUI or CBC configuration available to enable this.

Do it via customization. create a custom field (eg. ZSUPPLIER_PO) on Asset Master. Use Fiori -Manage Your Solution.
Add a Substitution Rule - while saving the asset, post-acquisition, if PO number exists, read PO header vendor via standard API, then populate supplier or your custom field. Display Custom Field in asset master Fiori view.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Restrict Tasks created in the Manage My Timesheet app
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In our employee time report, we can see time submitted by users using cost center and activity types only. We only need them to charge on WBS element or non project related task types. Is it possible to limit them on creating tasks using Cost center and activity type only? And how can we do that?

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restrict Tasks created in the Manage My Timesheet app
entry.content: In order to restrict users from submitting time for particular tasks, the BAdI HCM: Timesheet Validations (HCM_CATS_V1_TS_VALIDATION) can be implemented. Please see the following SAP Help documentation for more information on this App Adaptability and Extensibility: Manage My Timesheet.

entry.title: Re: New Expert-Guided Implementation: Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI
entry.content:
Hi @alejandroazca ,

I would like to register to one of the sessions but unfortunately I'm not able to. When I click on the link, I'm just logged into my SF environment. I've also followed this video https://cdnapisec.kaltura.com/html5/html5lib/v2.91/mwEmbedFrame.php/p/1921661/uiconf_id/35919811/entry_id/1_59z2n8fc?wid=_1921661&iframeembed=true&playerId=kaltura_player_1195878186&entry_id=1_59z2n8fc but even with it I'm not able to do so. Could it be that SSO is causing issues for this? I've also used incognito window but I still have issues. I checked that my user is active and I'm receiving below error message when I try to do the last step explained in video

We couldn't log you in.

Please contact your system administrator.
Error: Invalid Login
Your account may be locked, inactive, or not included in the default user group. If your organization uses SSO, it may not be working correctly

entry.title: Re: Hello! to all members of community
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to SAP community Ronit! 

We're excited to have you here and I hope you have fun engaging with the people in the community. Make sure to check out our Manage Tags page. You can find lots of information about financial accounting there!

You can also look for your city among the Location Groups and join the events closest to you. If your city isn’t listed yet, you are more than welcome to start your own group.

entry.title: 🇧🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Mechelen, Belgium)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 19 September 2025 in Mechelen, Belgium  from 09:00 - 16:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location:  Duwijckstraat 17, 2500 Lier

The instructor is Andre Fischer

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Find the Bug 🐞 Formatting dates and times in Build Apps
entry.content:
Someone sent me a problem they were having in a private message, that made me chuckle.

To be honest, after 2 years of war and 2 weeks of intense war, and having to live out of a suitcase for a week in Athens because my plane was diverted and could not enter my country -- this was a fun, amusing diversion (it actually made me laugh).

Here's the message I received:

 

FORMAT_DATETIME_WITH_TIMEZONE(DATETIME("2025-04-03T02:00:00.000Z"), "MMM d, yyyy", "America/Chicago")

 

"I am using formula to format the date. Actually the output should be Apr 2, 2025, 9 pm. But I am getting Apr 3, 2025, 9 pm. How to solve this? I tried multiple ways. It's only changing time but not date. I used other timezone format as well. Please help. This is an issue in production."

After running the formula in my Build Apps and getting the same result, I assumed it was a bug in the tool (@Mari and @Pekka_Aaltonen, please forgive me). For the programmer, the obvious thing to think was that you asked for the date in the format April 2 (because it had to give the day before because the time zone they gave was minus 6 hours from UTC) and instead it was giving April 3. Why? Probably because the formula was too stupid to return the previous day's date.

It was supposed to show this:

But kept showing this:

But, alas, there was a perfectly logical explanation, but it was hidden because of the actual date and time used in the example. Another case of all-too-human error.

Can you find the bug?

P.S.: If you like this puzzle, wait for Devtoberfest in October. We'll have plenty more.

entry.title: NA
entry.content: NA

entry.title: Re: Sap project system integration with production order
entry.content:
The best approach depends on the mix of production demand scenarios you have.  

Is your production all driven from a project or how much is project driven demand vs. MTO, MTS, S&OP demand?

A Network is an order type just like a PP Order, so there is a lot of overlapping functionality.

If you use Networks to perform production rather than PP orders you are disconnecting the 2 orders types.

I think a better fit would be to use PS Networks to create a project schedule and production demand into MRP and let MRP create the Production Order.  With this you have all of you Production Orders in one place and can be managed more effectively.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community!
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP Community, Sudeep!

It’s great to have you joining ys. Congratulations on completing your PGDERP in SAP FICO and best of luck with your internship at Daffodils!

Make sure to explore the Manage Tags  page to dive deeper into SAP FICO discussions, and don’t forget to check out the Location Groups  to connect with others in your area. If there’s no group for Ahmedabad yet, you’re more than welcome to start one!

Wishing you a great journey of learning and collaboration here!

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Hamburg, Germany)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for  Thursday 25 September 2025 in Hamburg, Germany  from 09:00 - 16:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location: TBD

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: *** Live sessions *** SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA - Register now
entry.content:
In this live session, we will explore the concept and value of Fiori Lighthouse Apps—strategic, next-generation SAP Fiori applications designed to deliver a modern, intuitive, and intelligent user experience in SAP S/4HANA. We will highlight key Lighthouse Apps across lines of business, and demonstrate their capabilities.

Learning objectives
After participating in this live session, you will have an understanding of:

  • What SAP Fiori Lighthouse Apps are and how they differ from classic Fiori apps
  • How Lighthouse Apps deliver innovation through intelligence and automation

Live Session schedule

Title Date / Time  
SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA
Aug 1, 2025, 08:00 - 10:00 GMT+2Register
SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA
Aug 1, 2025, 16:00 - 18:00 GMT+2Register

entry.title: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master data between two SAP systems via an IDOC flat file
entry.content:
I've got the following scenario which I want to run without custom program.

It currently fails on the last step, I hope you can propose a standard way to automate.

I've got two production S/4HANA systems C01 and D01, the master data from C01 must be replicated automatically on D01 every X days.

  1. On the system C01, I run the program RBDSER01 to replicate GLMAST messages via the group GRP_FI, which creates several outbound IDOC targeting D01 (one IDOC per G/L master created or changed since the last execution, the information is stored in the change pointers). A receiver file port has been configured. This port writes to a text file on a shared directory.
  2. I run these IDOC via BD87, they are successfully written to the file.
  3. On the system D01, I run the program RSEINB00 to process this text file, which creates several inbound IDOC, but their processing fails with status 56 and message "EDI: Receiver port in control record is invalid" (EA602) due to the receiver port. 

One workaround could have been to run WE12 to copy the file and change the target receiver port to SAPD01 (this works), but I cannot run it automatically.

Isn't there a simple way to integrate this IDOC text file automatically without having to modify it? If not, is there another solution?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Connecting UI5 TypeScript Components locally
entry.content:
Hi,

this blog post is quite "old", but I used it to bind/use a TS SAPUI5 library in my application. The hint about the 'devDep..' in package.json was important. However, I still had issues using the library locally, even when using the ui5-middleware-ui5, as my app could not resolve the path. If you are struggling with the same problem, check the settings I configured in SAP BAS. These settings allow you to activate live reloading, meaning that when you change something in the library, your application will be refreshed automatically. This settings also enables you to use the library locally without building it every time or using server-static middleware to resolve the path from .js to .ts.

in manifest.json of the app:

"sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": false,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.120.0",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "xyz.glibutil": {
          "lazy": false
        }
      }
    }
 
in package.json of the app:
devDependencies": {
    "@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools": "^0.4.0",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@sapui5/types": "~1.135.0",
    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^7.1.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^7.1.1",
    "@ui5/cli": "^3.0.0",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.5",
    "typescript": "^5.1.6",
    "ui5-tooling-transpile": "^3.3.7",
    "xyz.glibutil": "file:../xyz.glibutil"
  },
 
ui5.yaml of the app:
specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: my.little.app
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: false # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            destination: XXX
            url: http://xxx
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path:
          - "webapp"
          - "../xyz.glibutil"
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        component: my.little.app
        ui5Theme: sap_horizon
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transpileDependencies: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
        excludePatterns:
          - /Component-preload.js
builder:
  customTasks:
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transpileDependencies: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true

entry.title: Re: Enable SSO for BTP platform users
entry.content:
Please look into this blog: Configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services and Micros... - SAP Community

It's for use with JOULE, but most of it can be used for any other application.

entry.title: Re: SAC/Seamless Planning – Transactions Recorded at High...
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil,

thank you very much for your answer. Just to clarify, did you mean that I should create a second hierarchy within my dimension where all elements are treated as leaf nodes? Or did I possibly misunderstand your suggestion? I tried to create a second hierarchy in the dimension, but I encountered the following error message: “You cannot use the current member as a parent.” Could you please provide more details on how exactly the alternative hierarchy should be structured? That would be really helpful. Thanks again for your support!

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for secure act 2.0 section 109 roll ove...
entry.content:
@joe_jones2,

We’re in the same boat!  We’ve looked at note 3365473, but the options are lacking:

  • WRNG_SKIP & SKIP if you want to make your catch-up employees mad
  • ERROR if you want to irritate your Payroll Department
  • WRNG_CALC and CALC if you want to flout the legislation

The SKIP options do move the would-be catch-up amount into wage type /TB8, so I suppose you could do something with that downstream in the payroll schema.  I hope SAP works on this a bit more to deliver a complete solution.  The Regulatory Change Manager hints at something to be delivered 8/1/25, so maybe there’s hope…  https://app.main.regulatory-change-manager.cloud.sap/index.html#/detail/GSREQAMER-11581_8

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check - How to Update Your Implementation of SAP Readiness Check to Support SAP S
entry.content:
Hi,
i am unable to run the program. since it does not exist in my S4 ssyem.
can any one help me to check further?

 



Thanks in advance.

Reg,
Prabhat

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content: Hi @MSo the help link lists the steps for pulling data from leading Identity source (Entra ID)  using SAP IPS to the target BTP solution.  This works well including the substitution of the Global User ID in IAS directory using Global user ID from Entra ID.   The issue is with using Entra ID provisioning engine to achieve the same result - there is no direct involvement of IPS here.

entry.title: Relationship of product id with pricelist in sap crm. how to check this in config level
Re: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content:
Hi @JoanneDuggan ,

Yes, you can attach a document in AP. At the bottom of your app - Create supplier Invoice - you have "Attachments" option. Use that to upload pdf or image. The attachment will get saved when you post the document. Attachment will also be retained even though you haven't posted the invoice yet.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master dat...
entry.content:
It will only be handled automatically on import if the receiver port is blank, which means you would need to alter what is output somehow, or adjust the file after it is generated - 

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Cannot make ITPPM_PROJECT UI Elements Invisible
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing ChaRM and we are not using ITPPM. We have therefore attempted to hide the ITPPM Project related fields that are available by standard in Standard Change documents (ZMSG), but even after adjusting the configuration as required (see attachment for details), the fields still appear.

Requesting your support and guidance.

Thanks

entry.title: Send OData Batch Requests in SAP Build Apps (JSON)
entry.content:
Many people have asked about sending OData batch requests via SAP Build Apps. It can be done, if your API accepts JSON-formatted payloads for batch requests.

Just use the HTTP Destination Request flow function.

Then set the following:

  • Destination: Create an integration with your destination that points to your API (no need to specify any entities).
  • Method: POST
  • Body Type: JSON
  • Request Body: Just define a page variable with the JSON schema you need, and set the values with a Set Page Variable flow function. More on this below.
  • Path: /$batch
  • Headers: Content-Type = application/json

Request Body

The request body would be something like this (here we are create new records):

{
    "requests": [
        {
            "id": "1",
            "method": "POST",
            "url": "/Orders",
            "headers": {
                "content-type": "application/json; odata.metadata=minimal; odata.streaming=true",
                "odata-version": "4.0"
            },
            "body": {
                "customer": "JSON1",
                "date": "2021-06-30T00:00:00Z",
                "status": "Initiated",
                "total": 99,
                "currency_code": "USD"
            }
        },
        {
            "id": "2",
            "method": "POST",
            "url": "/Orders",
            "headers": {
                "content-type": "application/json; odata.metadata=minimal; odata.streaming=true",
                "odata-version": "4.0"
            },
            "body": {
                "customer": "JSON2",
                "date": "2021-06-30T00:00:00Z",
                "status": "Initiated",
                "total": 100,
                "currency_code": "USD"
            }
        }
    ]
}

For my test, I already had the JSON above so I just put it in a JavaScript component, parsed it, and output it.

But you could create a page variable, and in your app populate the variable with your data. Then you could use this page variable for the request body.

Unfortunately, I could not find a way to use the more traditional multipart/mixed payload format from SAP Build Apps. If you can find a way, let us know.

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025
entry.content:
Hi @cecilysorenson,
Are there plans for when the "What's New in SAP BTP – Q2 2025" blog will be released?

entry.title: Re: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content: Thanks for this Divyesh - I don't see that functionality on my screen

entry.title: Re: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content: I only see Check, Simulate, Hold, Park, Save as Completed & Cancel

entry.title: Re: How to connect my BTP Trial ABAP Account to BAS Trial...
entry.content:
In BAS, go to terminal and use following command, you can get the cloud foundry URL from your BTP account.

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the quick response.

The solution is working fine for me now.

I have below questions:

1.I commented the local class definition & implementation & where used code.Didnt delete it.

2.Do we need to Transport 3579012 to Production environment or higher environmnents ?

3.Delete the generated transport object. I didnt do this step, but still it works. I selected 'No Transport' when creating BC maintenance object. is this step required in that case ?

Thanks,

Manjunath
